angles.title: The Security Triage Nobody Admits To

tension.accepted_narrative: Companies invest in protecting all of their people and assets equally.
tension.ground_truth: Budget and staffing reality force security leaders to triage coverage long before they admit it publicly.

supporting_quotes.quote: We have eighty thousand employees. Realistically, we have budget to monitor maybe fifty of them properly.
Adding another analyst just means more people doing the same manual screening.

angles.hook_suggestion: Your company says it protects its people. The budget says it protects fifty of them.

angles.title: More Headcount Does Not Fix a Broken Workflow

tension.accepted_narrative: When compliance workload rises, the answer is to hire another analyst.
tension.ground_truth: Buyers keep describing process design failures that extra headcount only makes more expensive.

supporting_quotes.quote: We just have more hands doing the same manual work.

angles.hook_suggestion: If hiring one more analyst does not reduce the chaos, you do not have a staffing problem.

angles.title: Customers Keep Calling It Audit Prep Mode

tension.accepted_narrative: The category talks about continuous compliance maturity and unified visibility.
tension.ground_truth: Buyers describe their lives with operational survival language that reveals how episodic and brittle the work still is.

supporting_quotes.quote: My team has two modes: audit prep mode and catching up on everything we ignored during audit prep mode.

angles.hook_suggestion: The phrase buyers keep using is not continuous compliance. It is audit prep mode.
